design_philosophy.core_loop: Select team, manage roster, pitch/bat/field through innings, progress through season or single games
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time execution of pitching and batting against opponent AI with realistic baseball mechanics
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Managing and playing as a professional baseball team with authentic MLB rosters and atmosphere
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes fast-paced, action-filled baseball experience over deep simulation—trades some realism for arcade accessibility and party-game appeal

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can select difficulty implicitly through opponent selection and gameplay modes (single game, full season, playoffs, Home Run Derby)

progression.upgrade_system: Create-a-player customization allowing roster building and player transfers under player control
progression.unlock_mechanism: Big League Challenge Mode progression unlocks additional content
replayability_hooks: Full season play with team management

world_building_techniques: Authentic MLB team rosters and player names
Commentary by professional broadcasters (Jim Hughson, Buck Martinez on PS1; Sean McDonough on PS2)
Licensed music featuring Vitamin C's cover of 'I Know What Boys Like'
Real-world baseball context (Jason Giambi cover star)

faction_indicators: Player roster management and team loyalty
environmental_storytelling: MLB stadiums and authentic team branding
Player statistics and performance metrics reflecting real MLB data

sources_used: wikipedia - Triple Play Baseball (2001 version article, noting it as final PS/Windows release but first PS2 entry)
TODO: Note: Wikipedia source provided data for 2001 PS/PS2/Windows versions, not the 1995 Genesis version specified in game_id. Cross-platform Triple Play series details available but Genesis-specific documentation unavailable in provided sources. Data extracted reflects general series design philosophy where applicable.
